speaker: John the Baptist
quote_text: One more powerful than I is coming
prompt_context: At the Jordan, before Jesus began his public ministry, the wilderness preacher spoke of someone greater who would come after him.
question: Who said that one more powerful than he was coming after him?

speaker: Voice from Heaven
quote_text: You are my beloved Son
prompt_context: At Jesus' baptism, as he came up out of the water, a heavenly voice affirmed his identity and pleasure.
question: Who declared at Jesus' baptism that he was the beloved Son?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: The kingdom of God has come near
prompt_context: After John's arrest, Jesus began preaching in Galilee with a message of fulfillment, repentance, and faith.
question: Who proclaimed that the kingdom of God had come near and called people to repent and believe?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Follow me
prompt_context: By the Sea of Galilee, Jesus called fishermen away from their nets with a promise about a new kind of work.
question: Who told Simon and Andrew to follow him and become fishers of people?

speaker: Unclean Spirit
quote_text: I know who you are
prompt_context: In the synagogue at Capernaum, a demon cried out in recognition when Jesus taught with authority.
question: Who cried out to Jesus, saying that he knew who he was, the Holy One of God?

speaker: Leper
quote_text: If you choose, you can make me clean
prompt_context: A man with leprosy knelt before Jesus and appealed to both his power and willingness to heal.
question: Who told Jesus that if he chose, he could make him clean?

speaker: Scribes
quote_text: Who can forgive sins but God alone?
prompt_context: When Jesus told the paralyzed man his sins were forgiven, the religious teachers reasoned critically in their hearts.
question: Who questioned who could forgive sins but God alone after the paralytic was lowered before Jesus?

speaker: Scribes of the Pharisees
quote_text: Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?
prompt_context: At Levi's house, religious observers objected to Jesus sharing a meal with the wrong sort of people.
question: Who asked the disciples why Jesus ate with tax collectors and sinners?

speaker: People
quote_text: Why do John's disciples and the Pharisees fast?
prompt_context: Observers compared Jesus' circle to other religious groups and asked about differences in practice.
question: Who asked Jesus why his disciples did not fast like John's disciples and the Pharisees?

speaker: Pharisees
quote_text: Why are they doing what is not lawful?
prompt_context: When Jesus' disciples plucked grain on the Sabbath, the Pharisees challenged the action.
question: Who asked Jesus why his disciples were doing what was not lawful on the Sabbath?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good?
prompt_context: In the synagogue, before healing the man with the withered hand, Jesus challenged the silence of his opponents.
question: Who asked whether it was lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do harm?

speaker: Unclean Spirits
quote_text: You are the Son of God
prompt_context: Whenever the demonic spirits saw Jesus, they fell down before him and shouted what they recognized.
question: Who cried out to Jesus, "You are the Son of God"?

speaker: Scribes
quote_text: He has Beelzebul
prompt_context: Religious teachers from Jerusalem tried to explain Jesus' power by accusing him of alliance with evil.
question: Who said that Jesus had Beelzebul and cast out demons by the ruler of demons?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Do you not understand this parable?
prompt_context: After telling the parable of the sower, Jesus began explaining to his inner circle why his stories mattered.
question: Who asked the disciples whether they understood the parable of the sower?

speaker: Disciples
quote_text: Teacher, do you not care?
prompt_context: When a violent storm threatened to swamp the boat, the disciples woke Jesus in alarm.
question: Who asked Jesus whether he cared that they were perishing in the storm?

speaker: Unclean Spirit
quote_text: What have you to do with me?
prompt_context: In the country of the Gerasenes, the possessed man ran to Jesus and the unclean spirit spoke through him.
question: Who cried out to Jesus not to torment him in the Gerasene region?

speaker: Demon Called Legion
quote_text: My name is Legion
prompt_context: When Jesus asked for the name of the spirit in the possessed man, the answer emphasized their number.
question: Who answered Jesus by saying, "My name is Legion"?

speaker: Jairus
quote_text: My little daughter is at the point of death
prompt_context: A synagogue leader begged Jesus urgently to come lay hands on his child.
question: Who pleaded with Jesus because his little daughter was near death?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Who touched my clothes?
prompt_context: After power went out from him in the crowd, Jesus stopped and asked a question that led to a hidden testimony.
question: Who asked, "Who touched my clothes?" after the woman with bleeding was healed?

speaker: Disciples
quote_text: You see the crowd pressing around you
prompt_context: Hearing Jesus ask who touched him in a packed crowd, the disciples responded with puzzlement.
question: Who replied to Jesus that he could see the crowd pressing around him and yet ask who touched him?

speaker: People from Jairus's House
quote_text: Your daughter is dead
prompt_context: While Jesus was still speaking to the healed woman, messengers arrived with devastating news for Jairus.
question: Who told Jairus that his daughter was dead and that he should no longer trouble the teacher?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Do not fear, only believe
prompt_context: Immediately after the report from Jairus's house, Jesus spoke courage into the synagogue leader's grief.
question: Who told Jairus, "Do not fear, only believe"?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: The child is not dead but sleeping
prompt_context: At Jairus's house, after the report that the girl had died, Jesus spoke before raising her.
question: Who said that Jairus's daughter was not dead but sleeping?

speaker: People of Nazareth
quote_text: Where did this man get these things?
prompt_context: In his hometown synagogue, Jesus' hearers stumbled over his wisdom because they knew his ordinary background.
question: Who asked where Jesus got these things when he taught in Nazareth?

speaker: Herod
quote_text: John, whom I beheaded, has been raised
prompt_context: Hearing reports about Jesus, Herod interpreted them through the guilt of his past crime against John.
question: Who said that John, whom he had beheaded, had been raised?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Come away by yourselves to a deserted place
prompt_context: After the disciples returned from their mission, Jesus invited them to rest because many were coming and going.
question: Who told the disciples to come away by themselves to a deserted place and rest?

speaker: Disciples
quote_text: Are we to go and buy bread?
prompt_context: Faced with feeding a great crowd in a deserted place, the disciples questioned the practicality of Jesus' instruction.
question: Who asked Jesus whether they should go and buy bread for the crowd to eat?

speaker: Pharisees
quote_text: Why do your disciples not live according to the tradition?
prompt_context: Observing the disciples eat with unwashed hands, the Pharisees and scribes challenged Jesus over ritual purity.
question: Who asked Jesus why his disciples did not live according to the tradition of the elders?

speaker: Syrophoenician Woman
quote_text: Even the dogs under the table eat the children's crumbs
prompt_context: When Jesus tested her with a hard saying about children and dogs, the Gentile mother answered with humble persistence.
question: Who replied to Jesus that even the dogs under the table eat the children's crumbs?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Why does this generation seek a sign?
prompt_context: After the Pharisees came to argue with him and test him by seeking a sign from heaven.
question: Who asked why that generation was seeking a sign?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Why are you talking about having no bread?
prompt_context: In the boat, the disciples misunderstood Jesus' warning about leaven, so he questioned their dullness.
question: Who asked the disciples why they were discussing that they had no bread?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: Who do you say that I am?
prompt_context: Near Caesarea Philippi, after asking what others said about him.
question: Who asked the disciples who they said he was?

speaker: Peter
quote_text: You are the Messiah
prompt_context: When Jesus asked the disciples at Caesarea Philippi who they said he was, one disciple answered for the group.
question: Who told Jesus, "You are the Messiah"?

speaker: Peter
quote_text: It is good for us to be here
prompt_context: At the transfiguration, after seeing Jesus with Moses and Elijah.
question: Who told Jesus at the transfiguration that it was good for them to be there?

speaker: Voice from the Cloud
quote_text: This is my beloved Son; listen to him
prompt_context: At the transfiguration, a cloud overshadowed those present and a heavenly voice spoke again.
question: Who said from the cloud, "This is my beloved Son; listen to him"?

speaker: Father of the Boy
quote_text: I believe; help my unbelief
prompt_context: When Jesus told a desperate father that all things are possible for one who believes, the father answered honestly.
question: Who cried out, "I believe; help my unbelief"?

speaker: Disciples
quote_text: Why could we not cast it out?
prompt_context: After Jesus healed the boy with the unclean spirit, the disciples asked privately about their failure.
question: Who asked Jesus privately why they could not cast the spirit out?

speaker: John
quote_text: We saw someone casting out demons in your name
prompt_context: One disciple reported to Jesus about an outsider doing powerful works without belonging to their immediate group.
question: Who told Jesus about someone casting out demons in his name who was not following them?

speaker: Pharisees
quote_text: Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?
prompt_context: Trying to test Jesus in Judea, the Pharisees raised a question about marriage and divorce.
question: Who asked Jesus whether it was lawful for a man to divorce his wife?

speaker: James and John
quote_text: Grant us to sit, one at your right hand and one at your left
prompt_context: On the road toward Jerusalem, two brothers asked Jesus for places of honor in his glory.
question: Who asked Jesus for the seats at his right and left in glory?

speaker: Bartimaeus
quote_text: Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me
prompt_context: As Jesus left Jericho, a blind beggar began shouting with messianic recognition from the roadside.
question: Who cried out, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me"?

speaker: Chief Priests and Scribes and Elders
quote_text: By what authority are you doing these things?
prompt_context: In the temple after the disruption of commerce, the leaders confronted Jesus about his authority.
question: Who asked Jesus by what authority he was doing these things?
option_b: Chief Priests and Scribes and Elders
correct_answer: Chief Priests and Scribes and Elders

speaker: Pharisees and Herodians
quote_text: Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar?
prompt_context: Trying to trap Jesus in his words, an unusual alliance came with a politically loaded question.
question: Who tried to trap Jesus with a question about paying taxes to Caesar?

speaker: Sadducees
quote_text: Teacher, Moses wrote for us
prompt_context: The Sadducees, who denied the resurrection, presented Jesus with a marriage case meant to expose what they thought was absurdity.
question: Who raised the question about the woman who had seven husbands in the resurrection?

speaker: Scribe
quote_text: Which commandment is the first of all?
prompt_context: After hearing Jesus answer well in debate, one scribe approached with a question about what mattered most.
question: Who asked Jesus which commandment was first of all?

speaker: Some
quote_text: Why was the ointment wasted in this way?
prompt_context: When a woman anointed Jesus with costly perfume at Bethany, some present criticized what they saw as extravagance.
question: Who complained that the ointment had been wasted when the woman anointed Jesus?

speaker: Peter
quote_text: Even though all become deserters, I will not
prompt_context: At the supper, after Jesus predicted scattering, Peter insisted that his own loyalty would not fail.
question: Who told Jesus that even if all others deserted him, he would not?

speaker: Jesus
quote_text: My soul is very sorrowful, even to death
prompt_context: In Gethsemane, Jesus drew his closest disciples deeper into the garden and revealed the weight of his anguish.
question: Who said, "My soul is very sorrowful, even to death" in Gethsemane?

speaker: High Priest
quote_text: Are you the Messiah, the Son of the Blessed One?
prompt_context: During the night trial, after many conflicting testimonies, the high priest put the decisive question directly to Jesus.
question: Who asked Jesus whether he was the Messiah, the Son of the Blessed One?

speaker: Young Man at the Tomb
quote_text: Do not be alarmed
prompt_context: When the women entered the tomb early on the first day of the week, a young man in white spoke to them about Jesus.
question: Who told the women at the tomb not to be alarmed and that Jesus had been raised?
